content.full_text:
April 12, 1990

Linda Moureaux
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Filing Date for Form 721/Statement of Economic Interests

Dear Ms. Moureaux:

I am writing to confirm our phone conversation of a couple of weeks ago concerning the filing date for Form 721 for a candidate for a county board of supervisors. I am the treasurer of Roberta Achtenberg's Campaign for the Board of Supervisors in the City and County of San Francisco. Her election is in November 1990.

I realize that you may not recall the details of our conversation given that you receive numerous inquiries concerning a variety of issues. I will briefly explain the issue we discussed.

Because I learned that several officeholders and some candidates were required to file Form 721 by March 9, 1990, I became alarmed that my candidate should have also filed that form. However, the instructions to Form 721 (page 8, attached) provide that the form does not have to be filed until the final filing date for the declaration of candidacy. Accordingly, the deadline for filing is dependent on the meaning of the term "final filing date for the declaration of candidacy."

The San Francisco Registrar of Voters informed me that the final filing date for the declaration of candidacy for the November 1990 election for Supervisors is not until August 17 or 18, 1990. He confirmed that the Registrar's office did not believe that Form 721 was due for a supervisor candidate until that date.

When you and I talked on the phone (around March 20th), you concurred with that opinion. Simply, Form 721 is not due until the final date on which the declaration candidacy can be filed. In this instance, that date is not until August.

If your understanding of the rule is contrary to that set forth herein, please let me know as soon as possible.

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Yours truly,

[signature]
Richard K. Grosboll
